sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 3.59 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.3)

statement.latex: Let$H$be an insoluble minimal normal subgroup of a finite group$G$, and suppose that$H$has cyclic Sylow$p$-sub\-groups for every prime$p$dividing$|G : H|$. Prove that$H$has at least one complement in$G$. { L.\,A.\,She\-met\-kov} This was proved (S.\,A.\,Syskin, Siberian Math.~J., { 12} (1971), 342--344; \ L.\,A.\,She\-met\-kov, Soviet Math. Dokl., { 11} (1970), 1436--1438).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
